meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Overcome Project Stagnation
content.seo_description: Learn how to use analytics setup to solve 'can't finish projects' syndrome. A founder-focused guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to drive revenue.
content.answer_block: Most founders fail to ship because they lack feedback loops. By integrating lean analytics early, you transform 'gut feelings' into data-driven pivots. Use Antigravity to instrument key user events and Claude Code to automate the deployment. This creates the visibility needed to validate your MVP and finish your build.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is filled with half-finished codebases, abandoned because the founder lost the signal in the noise. You feel the stagnation—weeks pass, features bloat, and the 'done' line recedes into the distance. It isn't a lack of discipline; it’s a lack of feedback. Without knowing how real users interact with your MVP, you are flying blind, building features that don't move the needle.

True velocity comes from evidence. When you instrument your application with precise analytics, you stop guessing and start engineering solutions that actually generate revenue. This guide provides the technical scaffolding to bridge the gap between 'in-progress' and 'live', using modern AI tools to accelerate your workflow.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the one user action that correlates with revenue. This prevents scope creep by ensuring you only track what matters.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS Product Architect. Identify the top 3 event triggers for a B2B SaaS MVP that directly correlate to trial-to-paid conversion. Create a schema for these events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a JSON schema for user events including signup_complete, feature_usage_key, and subscription_started'

steps.title: Instrument the User Journey
steps.description: Inject tracking hooks into your core workflow. We focus on the 'Aha!' moment to ensure we aren't just tracking visits, but actual value delivery.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a lightweight tracking utility for a Next.js application that handles event buffering and batching to avoid blocking the main thread.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate the tracking utility into the user authentication and checkout flow components'

steps.title: Automate Data Pipelines
steps.description: Manual data analysis is a time sink. Connect your events to a dashboard that updates in real-time to keep you accountable to your launch date.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a serverless function that consumes raw event logs and transforms them into a summary report sent to a Slack channel every 24 hours.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Deploy the serverless function to AWS Lambda and configure the environment variables for Slack integration'

steps.title: Implement Kill-Switch Validation
steps.description: If a feature isn't being used, delete it. Use your analytics to identify 'dead weight' code that is preventing you from shipping the core product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the usage logs for the last 7 days and flag features with less than 5% adoption for potential deprecation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze current event logs and generate a report identifying underutilized features'

steps.title: Close the Feedback Loop
steps.description: Use the data to ship iterative updates. This keeps momentum high by proving your product is solving real problems.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on the conversion drop-off points in our analytics, suggest 3 UI/UX improvements to the onboarding flow to increase retention.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the onboarding component based on the suggested UI/UX improvements'

pro_tips: Track 'time-to-first-value' (TTFV) as your primary metric; if users don't hit value in under 2 minutes, you will never finish a profitable project.
Avoid tracking everything; if you track 100 events, you'll act on zero. Focus on the 3 that drive revenue.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your analytics hooks; bad data is worse than no data.
Set up a 'Ship-or-Pivot' alert; if your primary metric doesn't move after 14 days of traffic, force yourself to change the product direction.
Use Antigravity to generate mock data so you can test your dashboard before you even have a single user.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, commit to a 7-day 'Shipping Sprint'. Use the data from your dashboard to make one product adjustment every 48 hours to improve your conversion rate.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark. Use data to validate your vision, cut the feature bloat, and finally get your product into the hands of paying customers. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
